title: Jedi Quest: The Shadow Trap
content:
{{Top|rwm|imp|italics=1}} {{Book |image=[[File:The_Shadow_Trap.jpg]] |title=''Jedi Quest: The Shadow Trap'' |author=[[Jude Watson]] |cover artist=*[[Alicia Buelow]] *[[David Mattingly]] |illustrator= |editor= |publisher=[[Scholastic]] |early release= |release date=[[May]] [[2003]]''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' |intl release= |rereleased= |media type=Paperback |pages=125 |isbn=0439339227 |isbn2= |isbn3= |timeline=[[25 BBY/Legends|25 BBY]] |series=[[Star Wars: Jedi Quest (novel series)|''Star Wars: Jedi Quest'']] |preceded by=[[Jedi Quest: The School of Fear|''The School of Fear'']] |followed by=[[Jedi Quest: The Moment of Truth|''The Moment of Truth'']] }} '''''Jedi Quest: The Shadow Trap''''' is the sixth in a [[Star Wars: Jedi Quest (novel series)|series]] of young reader novels that chronicle the adventures of [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] between [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']] and [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Star Wars'': Episode II ''Attack of the Clones'']]. ==Publisher's summary==
'''Obi-Wan Kenobi and Anakin Skywalker.'''
'''Master and apprentice.'''
'''Chosen by fate. Destined for conflict.'''
''The planet of Mawan has been decimated by war and the resulting chaos. The population has taken refuge underground, while three greedy crimelords battle over food and fuel on the planet's surface. Only one group has a chance at securing peace—the Jedi''. ''But what if there's something even more sinister behind the chaos? What if the planet is really a trap, with many innocent lives held in the balance?'' ''What if, in order to save Mawan, a Jedi must die?'' ==Plot summary== Unsettling [[Force vision/Legends|visions]] of the future trouble [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] as he accompanies [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Yaddle/Legends|Yaddle]] to [[Mawan/Legends|Mawan]], a lawless [[Planet/Legends|world]] ruled by competing [[Crime lord/Legends|crime groups]] that have forced the innocent population underground. The [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] are led into a trap by their old foe, [[Granta Omega]], who is secretly running one of the crime groups. To prevent Granta from launching a [[Bioweapon/Legends|biological attack]] on the populace, Yaddle [[Death/Legends|sacrifices]] herself by blocking the path of a [[Weapon/Legends|weapon]] with her body. Anakin blames himself for Yaddle’s death, for in her sacrifice, she rescues him from Omega’s clutches. Though Omega escapes, Obi-Wan learns the truth behind their persistent enemy: he is the son of [[Xanatos]], [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn’s]] late fallen apprentice. ==Continuity== A change in the roster of the [[Jedi Council/Legends|Jedi Council]] from [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|Episode I]] ([[1999]]) to [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|Episode II]] ([[2002]]) allowed the intervening stories of the [[Star Wars Legends|Expanded Universe]] to eliminate prominent [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Masters]] in a dramatic fashion. The comic book ''[[Star Wars: Zam Wesell]]'' had [[Yarael Poof/Legends|Yarael Poof]] [[Death/Legends|sacrifice]] himself to save [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] from a [[Force artifact/Legends|Force talisman]] [[Weapon/Legends|weapon]], while [[Yaddle/Legends|Yaddle]] sacrificed herself in ''Jedi Quest: The Shadow Trap''. Their vacant spots on the Council were filled by newcomers [[Coleman Trebor/Legends|Coleman Trebor]] and [[Shaak Ti/Legends|Shaak Ti]]. [[Gardulla Besadii the Elder/Legends|Gardulla The Hutt]] was said to be dead although she later appeared in the ''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars]]'' series. The planet [[Koba]] and its native [[Koban|Kobans]] were misspelled as "Koda" and "Kodans" respectively. ==Media== {{Mediacat|from=1|imagecat=Images from Jedi Quest: The Shadow Trap}} ===Editions===
